title: Journey to Riddles Diary
description: The Hogwarts Express is departing — and you have been chosen to board. Somewhere along this enchanted journey lies Tom Riddle's Diary, the second Horcrux, waiting to be found and destroyed. The path is hidden in plain sight, woven through tickets, timetables, and secret stations. Trust nothing at face value. Look deeper than what the naked eye can see. Only the most vigilant witch or wizard will reach the Diary. All aboard. The clock is ticking.
clues: A boarding pass carries more than just a name. What you see on the surface is never the whole story. Wizards have ways of hiding messages where Muggles would never think to look.
Every witch and wizard knows the most legendary platform in all of King's Cross. It is not found on any Muggle sign — yet millions know its name.
Muggles have a saying — the answer is in the details. A certain magical tool can strip away the disguise of any file and expose what is truly hiding inside it.

title: Goblet's False Magic
description: We discovered how Barty Crouch Jr. tampered with the Goblet of Fire! He wrote a Python script to encrypt the true champion's identity. The goblet remains silent on the surface, but it reveals its secrets to those who know the spell. Find the artifacts, intercept the transmission, follow the trail, and reverse the dark magic.
question: Can you piece together the hidden clues, decode the transmission, and decrypt the true champion's identity?
clues: The deepest magic is written in the invisible margins of the parchment. Find the hidden vault first, then read the goblet's inner runes to learn which keystrokes will awaken its voice.
The rhythmic tapping is not a word, but a map to a distant shore. The artifact you find there is enchanted by a common muggle spell of 64 blocks-strip it away to reveal the true seed.

Barty’s false magic relies on a predictable memory. A machine fed the exact same thought will always repeat its actions. To break the curse, remember the golden rule of bitwise alchemy: what is combined can be perfectly uncombined.

title: Darklord's Transmission
description: The Order of the Phoenix has intercepted a strange burst of magical network traffic flying over the Floo Network. The Death Eaters are getting smarter—they aren't just sending files, they are smuggling them out in pieces. We captured the traffic in an pcap file. Can you reconstruct the transmission, fix the dark curses placed upon it, and uncover the final prophecy?
question: Analyze the packet capture, reconstruct the hidden artifact, and decode the final prophecy.
clues: The Dark Mark leaves a trail. Filter out the mundane muggle traffic and look at what the dark wizards are querying. Stitch the strange subdomains together to rebuild their package.

The package is cursed and will not open, its true identity erased from its head. Open the file's raw memory—if you find an archive at the tail, you must restore the 'Magic Bytes' of an image at its crown.
A repaired image is only a facade. Use a carving tool to separate the image from the archive hidden within it, then translate the ancient muggle encoding of base 64 to read the prophecy.

title: Ministry's Redacted Report
description: A highly classified Ministry of Magic report regarding Sirius Black's escape has been leaked. At first glance, the document is heavily redacted and seemingly useless. However, Arthur Weasley insists that the Ministry uses embarrassingly outdated security enchantments to hide their true findings. Can you find the hidden ciphertext and discover the true author to decrypt the final flag?
question: Uncover the hidden text and identify the author to break the encryption.
clues: Sometimes the most important magic is the kind you cannot see. Try selecting the entire parchment to reveal ink that blends in with the paper.
A substitution cipher requires a key. Who wrote this report? Inspect the deep properties and metadata of the document to find the author's alias.
With the hidden ciphertext and the author name in hand, you have everything you need to reverse a classic Vigenere polyalphabetic cipher.

title: Owl Network Intercept
description: The Ministry of Magic has intercepted suspicious owl communications across Hogwarts. title: The Intercepted Patronus
description: Dumbledore's Army has been communicating through enchanted photographs. We intercepted this image of a Patronus charm. It looks normal to the naked eye, but Hermione suspects they are using a muggle technique called 'Least Significant Bit' (LSB) encoding to hide messages directly inside the image's pixels. Can you extract the hidden message and crack the dark wizard's cipher to retrieve the flag?
question: Extract the hidden data to find the flag.
clues: You won't find the answer by just looking at the image or its metadata. Every pixel has an RGB value, and the message is hiding in the lowest, most insignificant numbers. A steg tool might be able to see it.
Once you uncover the hidden text, you'll find a 32-character string. That length is a dead giveaway for a very common, very outdated hashing algorithm.
The hash is a one-way street, but it's a weak one. You don't need a supercomputer to reverse it. Try comparing it against a massive dictionary of known passwords.

Searching the standard registry will yield nothing but ordinary wood. The wandmaker has left a specific tool right under your nose; use it not for its intended purpose, but as a bridge to his deeper secrets.
Every magical function holds memories of the environment it was forged in. Seek the global knowledge attached to the very tool he provided you, and the vault will expose its hidden contents.
